objective: Build and evolve a distributed AI execution system with FastAPI, Redis (RQ), worker architecture, retry logic, priority queues, dead letter queue, and migrate from SQLite to PostgreSQL; prepare system for observability layer (Phase 3.3).
decisions: Do not rebuild Phase 2; perform audit, connect, validate approach
Remove duplicate Redis layers and standardize queue usage
Use executor.py as single source of execution logic
Implement job creation API with queue enqueue
Fix module import issues by running from project root
Fix SQLAlchemy schema mismatch by adding 'type' field in Job model
Resolve circular imports by restructuring base.py and init_db.py
Fix DetachedInstanceError by controlling DB session lifecycle
Parse payload using json.loads before execution
Add Dead Letter Queue for failed jobs
Limit SQLite usage to single worker due to concurrency issues
Migrate database from SQLite to PostgreSQL for production readiness
Use existing PostgreSQL user (odoo) instead of assuming postgres
Set database credentials explicitly and align connection string
Maintain strict execution discipline and no simplification rule
built_components: Job status lifecycle management (queued, running, retrying, completed, failed)
Job status API endpoint for observability (basic)
inputs: User system architecture and directory structure
Forced failure injection in AI service
outputs: Retry attempts and retry count tracking
Failed jobs moved to Dead Letter Queue
API responses with job_id and status
notes: System has evolved from single-node SQLite-based execution to distributed multi-worker architecture with PostgreSQL. Retry, DLQ, and priority queues are fully operational. Observability layer (Prometheus + Grafana) is designed but not yet implemented. System is stable and ready for Phase 3.3 execution.
